document_title: Rough Volatility Visual Lab — Technical Report
report_subtitle: Technical report · synthetic data only · seed {seed} · configuration {fingerprint}
footer: Generated locally from saved, provenance-stamped experiment artifacts. No market data, remote scripts, or external services are used.
section.conceptual-map: From rough paths to option skew and order flow
section.fbm-path-comparison: Lower H changes local texture, not simply volatility
section.local-zoom: Roughness remains visible under a local zoom
section.increment-acf: Short-lag increments become anti-persistent below H=1/2
section.structure-functions: Structure-function slopes recover the H ordering
section.estimator-bias: Longer samples reduce, but do not erase, estimator bias
section.ou-versus-fou: Rough log-volatility differs locally from ordinary OU
section.rough-bergomi-paths: Rough Bergomi creates abrupt variance dynamics
section.heston-comparison: The Markovian benchmark responds differently to shared shocks
section.terminal-distributions: Model dynamics change return and realized-variance distributions
section.iv-smiles: Smile shape varies jointly with model and maturity
section.iv-surface: The implied-volatility surface concentrates structure at short maturities
section.atm-skew-term: Smaller H produces stronger short-maturity ATM skew
section.skew-scaling: Finite-maturity skew follows the predicted power-law direction
section.hawkes-events: Near-critical Hawkes flow clusters into bursts
section.order-flow-price: Signed event imbalance creates a clustered synthetic price
section.volatility-proxy: Clustered flow creates a rough-looking RV proxy
section.noise-bias: Noise and sampling choices materially shift estimated H
section.does-not-establish: What the experiments do not establish
section.limitations-next-steps: Limitations, robustness, and next research steps
